ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the customer */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Age range of the customer (e.g., 21-30) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the customer (e.g., Male, Female) */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the customer (e.g., Research, Student) */,
  "monthly_income_range" TEXT /* Monthly income range of the customer (e.g., <10,000, >40,000) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "product_type" TEXT /* Type of the product (e.g., ice cream, dessert) */,
  "flavours" TEXT /* Flavours available for the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "ingredients" TEXT /* Ingredients used in the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "rating_name" TEXT /* Name of the rating (e.g., 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the rating */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Taste, Quality) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "flavours" (
  "flavour_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each flavor */,
  "flavour_name" TEXT /* Name of the flavor (e.g., Vanilla, Chocolate) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the flavor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("flavour_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each preference */,
  "preference_name" TEXT /* Name of the preference (e.g., agree, neutral, disagree) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the preference */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recommendations" (
  "recommendation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recommendation */,
  "recommendation_name" TEXT /* Name of the recommendation (e.g., agree, neutral, strongly agree) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the recommendation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recommendation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, analyst, customer) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Role of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the channel (e.g., online, in-store, phone) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., store name, city) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)
column_names: Sex of the customer (e.g., Male, Female)
